curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: Vampires and killer clowns are fine, but you want to know what actually gives me the creeps? Escalators. Especially the ones in those old department stores where the metal teeth look a little too sharp at the bottom. I always do this little hop at the end, just in case.
quotes.speaker: Stephen King
quotes.context: Audience Q&A at the 2019 National Book Festival in Washington, D.C.
quotes.fake_note: Made this one up! The 'metal teeth' detail is exactly how he'd describe a mundane horror in one of his novels, but King's actual real-life phobias are spiders, snakes, and flying.

quotes.text: I have now come to the conclusion never again to think of marrying, and for this reason; I can never be satisfied with anyone who would be blockhead enough to have me.
quotes.speaker: Abraham Lincoln
quotes.context: A candidly self-mocking line from a young Lincoln's letter, poking fun at his own eligibility as a suitor.

quotes.text: We had this one microphone that kept picking up the local taxi dispatch. Right in the middle of a take for 'Blackbird' or something, you’d hear, 'Car 4, go to Piccadilly.' George Martin used to get so furious, but I always thought we should have just left it in the mix.
quotes.speaker: Paul McCartney
quotes.context: 2021 interview with Rick Rubin on the Hulu docuseries McCartney 3,2,1
quotes.fake_note: I completely fabricated this studio memory! It sounds exactly like one of Macca's cheerful, meandering anecdotes, but EMI Studios at Abbey Road were built like fortresses—a taxi radio bleeding into those mics would be technically impossible.

quotes.text: I am Al Gore, I used to be the next president of the United States
quotes.speaker: Al Gore
quotes.context: His self-deprecating opening line at a Harvard Kennedy School lecture, October 4, 2001, after losing the 2000 election

source.title: Harvard Gazette — Gore calls for unity

quotes.text: I was criticized for being too much concerned with the average Canadians. I can't help that; I am one of them!
quotes.speaker: John Diefenbaker
quotes.context: Canada's 13th prime minister, disarmingly turning a criticism into a humble admission about his own ordinariness.

quotes.text: These guys today have chefs and sleep pods and hyperbaric chambers. My rookie year, Moses Malone told me to eat a pizza before the game so I’d have enough weight to box out. I had two slices of pepperoni and grabbed fourteen rebounds. The science has changed, man.
quotes.speaker: Charles Barkley
quotes.context: Post-game coverage on TNT's Inside the NBA, May 2019
quotes.fake_note: Fabricated by yours truly! Moses Malone was a huge mentor to Charles, but the pizza advice is my own invention—Moses actually famously told him he was 'fat and lazy' to get him to lose weight, not gain it.
